label: Sixth Hour (Sexta)
description: The Byzantine Sixth Hour is sung at the sixth hour of the day (approximately noon). It consists of fixed psalmody (Psalms 53, 54, 90 LXX), the Trisagion prayer sequence, a troparion of the day, the Theotokion of the hour, and the Prayer of the Sixth Hour.

sections.label: Opening
sections.notes: The Sixth Hour opens with the Usual Beginning, as do all Byzantine offices.

items.text: The Priest blesses: Blessed is our God, always, now and ever and unto ages of ages.

items.label: The Usual Beginning
items.note: Trisagion prayers sequence. Resolved from sixth-hour-fixed.json by HorologionEngine.

sections.label: Fixed Psalmody
sections.notes: The Sixth Hour appoints three fixed psalms: 53, 54, and 90 (LXX). These are invariable on ordinary days.

items.label: Psalm 53 (LXX) — Save me, O God, by Thy Name
Psalm 54 (LXX) — Give ear to my prayer, O God
Psalm 90 (LXX) — He that dwelleth in the help of the Most High

sections.label: Trisagion Prayers
sections.notes: The Trisagion prayer sequence precedes the troparia.

sections.label: Troparia
sections.notes: The dismissal troparion of the day is sung. The Theotokion of the Sixth Hour follows.

items.label: Troparion of the Day
items.note: Variable troparion: resolved via Menaion resolver or weekday-theme rubric by HorologionEngine. Same infrastructure as Vespers, First Hour, and Third Hour troparion slot.

items.label: Theotokion of the Sixth Hour
items.note: Variable: tone-dependent Theotokion from the Octoechos Sixth Hour cycle. Resolved by HorologionEngine as explicit rubric stub at baseline; full text deferred pending Sixth Hour Octoechos data.

sections.label: Prayer of the Sixth Hour and Dismissal
sections.notes: The Prayer of the Sixth Hour concludes the fixed psalmody cycle. The office ends with the standard dismissal.

items.label: Prayer of the Sixth Hour
items.note: Fixed invariable prayer. Resolved from sixth-hour-fixed.json by HorologionEngine.

items.text:
Priest: Christ our true God, through the intercessions of His all-pure Mother, of all the Saints, have mercy on us and save us, as He is good and the Lover of mankind.
Choir: Amen.
